text:
Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily who lived in a cottage on the edge of a wood. Every morning, after breakfast, she would wear her pink bunny slippers and venture into the forest to meet her beloved tree friends.

Lily loved nature. The rustling leaves spoke to her, the sunbeams through the oak branches painted pictures on the walls of her thoughts. Her favorite tree was an old oak with broad branches and gnarled wisdom. It listened to her stories and laughed with her jokes.

One day, as Lily was frolicking among the silver-tip thrushes, she felt a sense of urgency. Her grandmother had suddenly fallen very ill. With courage bubbling inside her, she set out to visit her old lady.

The journey took her past hidden rivers and around majestic fells. Each corner told a tale—of a brave knight, of quiet woodland sprites, of times when the forest was just forest.
